violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Thermometers are lacking in display cases, reach-in units, and walk-in coolers. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The door to the outside, located in receiving, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the seafood 3-door reach-in observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gasket is to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the grates in the egg cooler and the shelving in the milk reach-in coolers, have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
The hand-washing sink in the produce area was blocked by (a pallet). A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Maintenace looked at the machine at the time of inspection. 3-bay sink is to be used for sanitization until machine is fixed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC), (chicken wings) were placed in sealed bag before being 41 DF. Condensation observed inside of the bag. Proper cooling methods were gone over with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the (condenser fans in meat backroom) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

A written consumer advisory is not provided on the seafood display case to inform the consumer of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2023

The rolling doors to the outside in receiving and produce, as well as the emergency exit door in produce, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2023
Cheese knife observed stored between the knife holder and the wall. Utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

Shelving unit and shelving brackets in the 3-door seafood reach-in are not in good repair. They are rusted and no longer able to be cleaned effectively. Shelving and brackets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the commercial mixer have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. The condenser fan in the prepared foods walk-in cooler has an accumulation of dust and debris. The shelving of the dairy reach-in units observed to have and accumulation of residue. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023
